back_translation: "For the kingdom of the heavens is like a master of a house, who went out at early dawn to hire workers to work in his vineyard.
After agreeing with the workers on a wage of one denarius per day, he sent them into his vineyard.
About nine o'clock in the morning, he went out and saw others standing in the marketplace without doing anything.
He said to those people, 'You also go to work in the vineyard; I will give you a fair wage.'
They went. He went out again about noon and three o'clock in the afternoon, and did the same way.
About five o'clock in the evening, he went out and found others standing, and said to them, 'Why have you been standing here without doing anything all day?'
They answered, 'Because no one has come to hire us.' He said to them, 'You also go to work in the vineyard.'
When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his head worker, 'Call the workers, and pay them their wages, beginning with the last ones up to the first.'
The ones who had been hired about five o'clock in the evening came, and each received one denarius.
When those who had been hired as the first group came, they thought they would receive more; but each of them also received one denarius.
Having received it, they began to complain against the master of the house,
grumbling, 'These who came later worked only one hour, but you have made them equal to us, who have borne the burden of the day and the scorching heat.'
But he answered one of them, 'Friend, I have not wronged you. Did you not agree with me on a price of one denarius?
Take what is yours and go. I desire to give this one who came later the same as I gave you.
Do I not have the right to do as I desire with what is mine? Or is your eye evil because I am good?'
In this manner, those who are last will become the first, and those who are first will instead be last."
While Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, He took the twelve disciples aside, and said to them along the way,
"Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the scribes; they will condemn Him to the punishment of death,
and will hand Him over to the Gentiles, so that He may be mocked, scourged, and crucified on the cross; and on the third day He will be raised up anew."
At that time, the mother of the sons of Zebedee came to Him together with her two sons. She prostrated herself in worship and humbly asked something from Him.
He asked her, "What do you desire?" She said to Him, "Please command that these two sons of mine sit, one at the right side of Your hand and the other at the left side of Your hand, in Your kingdom."
Jesus answered, "You do not know what you are asking. Can you drink the cup that I am about to drink?" They said to Him, "We both can."
He said to them, "It is true that you will drink My cup; but to sit at My right or My left is not Mine to give; it will be for those whom My Father has prepared it for."
When the other ten disciples heard this, they were angry at the two brothers.
But Jesus called them to Him and said, "You know that the rulers of the Gentiles use power to oppress them, and their great ones coerce and dominate them.
But among you it shall not be so. Whoever among you wishes to be great, that one must be your servant;
and whoever among you wishes to be first, that one must be your slave—
just as the Son of Man did not come to have people serve Him, but to serve others, and to give His life as a ransom in place of many."
While He and the disciples were leaving the city of Jericho, a large crowd followed Him.
Behold, there were two blind men sitting by the road. When they heard that Jesus was passing by, they cried out, "Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!"
The crowd forbade them to be silent; but they cried out all the louder, "Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!"
Jesus stopped and called them, saying, "What do you want Me to do for you?"
They said to Him, "Lord, let our eyes be opened."
Jesus had compassion, and touched their eyes; immediately they could see, and they followed Him.
